label: Gender, Race & Colonialism

sources.title: History 347 – The Caribbean and its Diasporas

app_prompts: Settler colonialism explainer: user picks a settler colonial context (North America, Australia, Palestine, South Africa) and traces the logic of elimination — how settler colonial states handled indigenous presence.
Intersectionality case study: user picks a historical figure or group and traces how gender, race, and class intersected to shape their experience — not one axis alone but all three together.

Caribbean diaspora timeline: user picks an island and traces the Atlantic slavery roots, emancipation, colonial rule, independence, and diaspora migration — who left, where they went, what they carried.

Feminist historiography explorer: user picks a historical topic (war, labor, religion, empire) and sees what questions feminist historians asked that conventional history missed — whose stories were left out.

Creolization and identity quiz: user reads about a Caribbean cultural practice — language, religion, food, music — and traces how it blended African, European, and indigenous elements under colonial conditions.
